e1_sentence: It has been narrated (through a different chain of transmitters) by Abu Ishaq  that:  A person said to Bara' (ibn 'Azib): "Abu Umara, did you flee on the Day of  Hunain?
It has been narrated (through a different chain of transmitters) by Abu Ishaq  that:  A person said to Bara' (ibn 'Azib): "Abu Umara, did you flee on the Day of  Hunain?
e2_sentence: He replied: "The Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him) did not  retreat.

e1_sentence: He replied: "The Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him) did not  retreat.
e2_sentence: What actually happened was that some hasty young men who were either  inadequately armed or were unarmed met a group of men from Banu Hawazin and  Banu Nadir who happened to be (excellent) archers

e1_sentence: He replied: "The Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him) did not  retreat.
e2_sentence: Bara' continued: When the battle grew fierce, we, by God, would seek  protection by his side, and the bravest among us was he who confronted the  onslaught and it was the Holy Prophet (may peace be upon him)
